doshas.name: Vata
physicalTraits: Thin, lean body frame with prominent joints and tendons
Dry, rough, cracked skin prone to early wrinkling
Dark or dusky complexion with cool skin temperature
Thin, dark, dry, coarse hair prone to split ends
Small, dark, dry eyes with thin eyelashes
Irregular appetite and variable digestion
Tendency to constipation and gas
Low stamina but quick energy bursts
Light, interrupted sleep; tendency to insomnia
Prominent veins and tendons
Small, thin teeth with tendency toward dental sensitivity
Irregular menstrual cycles with scanty flow (in women)
mentalTraits: Quick to learn but forgets easily
Creative, imaginative, and artistic
Enthusiastic, vivacious, and talkative
Tends toward anxiety, worry, and fear when imbalanced
Difficulty with decision-making; scattered thinking
Adaptable to change but can feel overwhelmed
Light sleeper with vivid, active dreams
Short-term memory is sharp; long-term memory weaker
Multi-tasking tendency; difficulty staying focused
balancingFoods: Warm, cooked, oily, and moist foods
Sweet, sour, and salty tastes (Madhura, Amla, Lavana)
Warm milk with ghee and honey
Root vegetables: sweet potato, carrot, beet
Cooked grains: rice, oatmeal, wheat
Ripe, sweet fruits: banana, mango, avocado, fig
Warming spices: ginger, cumin, cardamom, cinnamon
Sesame oil, ghee, olive oil
Nuts and seeds in moderation: almonds, sesame, walnut
Warm soups and stews
aggravatingFoods: Bitter, pungent, and astringent tastes in excess
Excessive fasting or irregular meal timing
doshas.seasonalVulnerability: Late autumn and winter (Hemanta and Shishira ritu); dry, cold, windy weather aggravates Vata
lifestyleGuidelines: Maintain consistent daily routine (Dinacharya)
Abhyanga (oil massage) daily with warm sesame oil
Gentle yoga: Yin yoga, slow Hatha, grounding postures
Avoid excessive travel and overstimulation
Early bedtime before 10 PM
Warm baths and steam therapy
Pranayama: Nadi Shodhana, Bhramari
Meditation: grounding, body scan techniques
Avoid cold environments and excessive wind exposure
Regular, warm, unhurried meals at fixed times

doshas.name: Pitta
physicalTraits: Medium build with good muscular development
Warm, soft, slightly oily skin prone to rashes and acne
Fair, reddish, or yellowish complexion with freckles or moles
Fine, soft hair; tendency toward premature greying or balding
Sharp, penetrating eyes; grey, green, or hazel coloured
Strong, regular appetite; intolerance to skipping meals
Loose stools; tendency toward diarrhea and hyperacidity
Strong metabolism and good digestion
Moderate sleep of medium duration; intense dreams
Prone to inflammation, fever, and infection
Excessive sweating with strong body odour
Regular, moderate menstrual flow with possible cramping
mentalTraits: Sharp intellect, analytical and organised
Strong leadership qualities and competitive drive
Articulate, persuasive, and focused communicator
Tendency toward anger, irritability, and criticism when imbalanced
Perfectionist with high standards
Good memory and concentration
Goal-oriented and ambitious
Courageous but can become domineering
balancingFoods: Cool, slightly dry, and moderately heavy foods
Sweet, bitter, and astringent tastes (Madhura, Tikta, Kashaya)
Cooling vegetables: cucumber, zucchini, leafy greens, asparagus
Sweet fruits: coconut, pomegranate, sweet mango, pear
Milk, ghee, and coconut oil
Cooked grains: basmati rice, barley, oats
Cooling herbs: coriander, fennel, mint, cardamom
Coconut water and herbal teas (rose, chamomile, licorice)
Sweet dairy: fresh yogurt diluted with water, buttermilk
aggravatingFoods: Sour and fermented foods in excess
doshas.seasonalVulnerability: Late spring and summer (Grishma ritu); hot, humid weather aggravates Pitta
commonDisorders: Acid reflux, gastritis, and peptic ulcers
Inflammatory skin conditions: acne, rosacea, urticaria
lifestyleGuidelines: Avoid direct sun exposure; work in cool environments
Cooling Abhyanga with coconut or sunflower oil
Moderate, competitive sports avoided; prefer swimming and walking
Cooling yoga: forward bends, moon salutation, Sitali pranayama
Meditation: compassion-based, visualization techniques
Adequate rest; avoid excessive overwork
Moonlight walks and time in nature
Avoid arguments and high-pressure deadlines
Regular meal timings; never skip lunch
Wear cool, light-coloured cotton clothing

doshas.name: Kapha
physicalTraits: Large, sturdy, well-built frame with tendency to gain weight
Thick, soft, oily, smooth, and lustrous skin
Fair or pale complexion with cold skin to touch
Thick, wavy, lustrous, oily hair
Large, attractive, calm eyes; often dark or blue
Slow but steady appetite; can skip meals easily
Slow but regular digestion; formed stools
Great stamina and endurance
Heavy, deep sleep; difficulty waking up
Strong immune system
Large, white, strong teeth
Regular, moderate to heavy menstrual flow
mentalTraits: Calm, steady, patient, and compassionate
Excellent long-term memory; slower to learn but retains well
Loyal, affectionate, and nurturing by nature
Tendency toward attachment, possessiveness, and stubbornness when imbalanced
Methodical and reliable worker
Slow to anger but holds grudges
Content and satisfied; may resist change
Emotionally stable and grounding for others
balancingFoods: Light, dry, warm, and spicy foods
Pungent, bitter, and astringent tastes (Katu, Tikta, Kashaya)
Light vegetables: cabbage, cauliflower, Brussels sprouts, spinach
Astringent fruits: pomegranate, cranberry, apple, pear
Barley, millet, corn, buckwheat
Warming spices: black pepper, ginger, turmeric, mustard
Honey (unheated) in small amounts
Legumes: split red lentils (masoor), mung dal
Herbal teas: ginger, tulsi, cinnamon
aggravatingFoods: Heavy, oily, cold, and sweet foods
Sweet, sour, and salty tastes in excess
Dairy products: cheese, yogurt, cream, ice cream
Wheat, white rice, and refined carbohydrates
Sweet fruits: banana, mango, avocado, dates
Overeating and eating out of schedule
doshas.seasonalVulnerability: Late winter and spring (Shishira and Vasanta ritu); cold, damp, cloudy weather aggravates Kapha
balancingHerbs: Vacha (Acorus calamus) — mental and respiratory clarity
lifestyleGuidelines: Regular vigorous exercise: jogging, cycling, aerobics
Dry powder massage (Udvartana) instead of oil massage
Early rising before 6 AM; avoid daytime sleep
Dynamic yoga: Sun salutation, Vinyasa, Kapalabhati pranayama
Stimulating meditation: breath awareness, mantra
Reduce attachment to possessions and relationships
Embrace new experiences and change
Avoid cold, damp environments
Intermittent fasting and light meals
Social engagement to counteract sluggishness

assessment.instructions: Answer each question based on your lifelong tendencies, not temporary or seasonal changes. Choose the option that most accurately describes you overall.

questions.question: What best describes your body frame and build?

options.vata: Thin, lean; difficulty gaining weight even with good appetite
options.pitta: Medium, proportionate; can gain or lose weight with moderate effort
options.kapha: Large, broad, stocky; gain weight easily, lose weight with difficulty

questions.question: How would you describe your skin texture and tendency?

options.vata: Dry, rough, thin; prone to chapping, cracking, and flaking
options.pitta: Warm, soft, slightly oily; prone to rashes, acne, and redness
options.kapha: Thick, smooth, oily, cool; prone to large pores and paleness

questions.question: What is your typical hair quality?

options.vata: Dry, thin, brittle, coarse; prone to split ends and breakage
options.pitta: Fine, soft, oily; tends toward early greying or premature thinning
options.kapha: Thick, oily, wavy, lustrous; slow to grey, minimal hair fall

questions.question: How is your appetite and hunger pattern?

options.vata: Irregular, variable; sometimes very hungry, sometimes forgets to eat
options.pitta: Strong, regular; irritable and impatient if meals are delayed
options.kapha: Low, steady; can comfortably skip meals without discomfort

questions.question: How would you describe your usual bowel habit?

options.vata: Irregular, tendency to constipation, hard dry stools, gas and bloating
options.pitta: Regular, often loose or soft; tendency toward diarrhea under stress
options.kapha: Regular, slow, well-formed, heavy stools; rarely constipated

questions.question: How is your sleep pattern?

options.vata: Light, interrupted, difficult to fall asleep; vivid or anxious dreams
options.pitta: Moderate, deep for a few hours; may wake hot or with intense dreams
options.kapha: Heavy, prolonged, deep; difficulty waking; feels groggy in the morning

questions.question: How do you typically respond to stress or difficulty?

options.vata: Become anxious, fearful, or overwhelmed; mind races and jumps
options.pitta: Become irritable, angry, and critical; want to control the situation
options.kapha: Withdraw, become stubborn or emotionally shut down; comfort eating

questions.question: How would you describe your memory and learning style?

options.vata: Quick to grasp new concepts but forgets quickly; scattered attention
options.pitta: Learns quickly, retains well; analytical and detail-oriented
options.kapha: Slow to learn but retains information for very long periods

questions.question: How do you handle cold weather?

options.vata: Very sensitive; feels cold easily; needs extra layers
options.pitta: Tolerates cold well; prefers cool weather over heat
options.kapha: Can tolerate cold but damp cold makes them feel sluggish and congested

questions.question: What best describes your energy level through the day?

options.vata: Variable, bursts of energy followed by sudden fatigue; unpredictable
options.pitta: Moderate to high; focused and purposeful; tires if goal is unclear
options.kapha: Steady, slow to start but sustained once going; low motivation in morning

questions.question: How do you characteristically speak and communicate?

options.vata: Fast, talkative, enthusiastic; topics jump around; may trail off
options.pitta: Clear, precise, direct, sometimes sharp or cutting
options.kapha: Slow, melodic, deliberate; chooses words carefully; rarely raises voice

questions.question: How do you typically sweat?

options.vata: Minimal sweating; skin often dry even after exertion
options.pitta: Profuse sweating with strong odour; even in moderate heat
options.kapha: Moderate, cool sweating; consistent without much variation

questions.question: How would you describe your natural body temperature tendency?

options.vata: Cold hands and feet; temperature fluctuates; dislikes cold
options.pitta: Warm or hot; feels hot easily; fan or cool air preferred
options.kapha: Cool, stable temperature; neither very hot nor very cold

questions.question: How would you characterise your physical activity preference?

options.vata: Light, quick movement; enjoys dance, walking, yoga; easily overstimulated
options.pitta: Competitive, intense; enjoys sports, challenges, measurable outcomes
options.kapha: Steady, endurance-based; prefers leisurely walks or swimming

questions.question: What best describes your eyes?

options.vata: Small, dark, darting, dry; tends to blink frequently
options.pitta: Medium, sharp, penetrating; grey, green or hazel; light sensitive
options.kapha: Large, calm, attractive, white sclera; watery or slightly moist

questions.question: How do you typically make decisions?

options.vata: Impulsive or indecisive; changes mind frequently; difficulty committing
options.pitta: Decisive, quick, goal-oriented; confident; can be rigid
options.kapha: Slow and deliberate; needs time; once decided, rarely changes mind

questions.question: How do you respond to change in routine or environment?

options.vata: Excited but unsettled; adapts quickly but may feel destabilised
options.pitta: Accepts change if it makes logical sense; resistant if not convinced
options.kapha: Dislikes change; prefers routine and familiarity; adapts slowly

questions.question: What is your financial tendency?

options.vata: Spends impulsively; difficulty saving; money comes and goes quickly
options.pitta: Planned, organised; spends on quality and goals; moderate savings
options.kapha: Conservative, saves well; reluctant to spend; attached to security

questions.question: How do you relate to others emotionally?

options.vata: Enthusiastic, friendly, flighty; many acquaintances, few deep bonds
options.pitta: Selective; loyal to those who meet standards; can be critical
options.kapha: Deeply loving, nurturing, loyal; forms strong lasting attachments

questions.question: What best describes your joints and flexibility?

options.vata: Thin joints, prominent knuckles; hypermobile; cracking sounds common
options.pitta: Medium joints; moderate flexibility; tenderness with inflammation
options.kapha: Large, well-padded joints; less flexible but stable and strong

profiles.name: Sama Agni
profiles.description: Ideal digestive state; food is digested at the right pace, producing Ojas (vital essence). No undue discomfort before, during, or after meals.
symptoms: Regular hunger at appropriate meal times
Complete, comfortable digestion without bloating or gas
Well-formed stools passed once or twice daily
Stable energy after meals without drowsiness
Absence of food cravings or aversions
diet: Maintain regular meal timings; three meals per day
Eat in a calm, seated environment without distraction
Include all six tastes in meals: Madhura, Amla, Lavana, Katu, Tikta, Kashaya
Avoid overeating; fill stomach two-thirds capacity
Warm or room-temperature cooked foods preferred
Fresh seasonal and locally grown produce
Minimal processed, preserved, or canned foods
Gentle spicing with cumin, coriander, turmeric, and ginger
herbs: Trikatu in small doses for maintenance
Triphala at bedtime for gentle cleansing
lifestyle: Light fasting once weekly to rest digestion

profiles.name: Vishama Agni
profiles.description: Erratic digestion; sometimes strong, sometimes weak; related to Vata imbalance affecting digestive function with unpredictable results.
symptoms: Irregular hunger; sometimes very hungry, sometimes no appetite
Abdominal cramping especially in lower abdomen
diet: Regular, fixed meal timings are essential
Warm, cooked, easily digestible foods
Mono-diet or simple two-ingredient meals temporarily
Warm water or cumin-fennel tea with meals
Avoid raw foods, cold meals, and carbonated drinks
Khichdi (rice and mung dal) as digestive reset meal
Small frequent meals rather than large ones
Ginger, cumin, ajwain (carom seeds) in cooking
Avoid beans, cruciferous vegetables temporarily
herbs: Dashamularishta — tonic for Vata and digestion
Triphala at night for bowel regulation
Ashwagandha to calm the nervous system
lifestyle: Strict daily routine including meal times
Regular sleep schedule to stabilise Vata
Avoid excessive travel, screen time, and overstimulation

profiles.name: Tikshna Agni
profiles.description: Overactive digestion; food digested too quickly; can burn through food and begin digesting the gut lining. Associated with Pitta imbalance.
symptoms: Excessive hunger; unable to tolerate hunger without irritability
Burning sensations in stomach and esophagus
Tendency to hyperacidity and peptic ulcer
diet: Sweet, cooling, and mildly oily foods
Bitter gourd, leafy greens, and cooling vegetables
Coconut milk, ghee, and cold-pressed oils
Room-temperature or slightly warm foods; avoid very hot meals
Milk with cardamom and saffron
Pomegranate, sweet lime, coconut water
Avoid spicy, fermented, sour, and fried foods
Barley water and fennel tea between meals
Avoid alcohol, coffee, and acidic foods
herbs: Avipattikar Churna — classical Pitta pacifier for hyperacidity
Shatavari — cooling and protective to gastric mucosa
Praval Pishti — calcium compound, reduces acidity
Chandanasava — cooling liver and stomach tonic
lifestyle: Avoid skipping meals; eat at regular times
Cool, relaxed meals in calm environment
Avoid high-intensity competitive exercise; prefer swimming
Spend time in nature, near water or in green spaces

profiles.name: Manda Agni
profiles.description: Underactive digestion; food takes too long to digest, leading to accumulation of Ama (toxins). Associated with Kapha imbalance and metabolic sluggishness.
symptoms: Low appetite; lack of interest in food even when hungry
Heaviness and fullness after small meals
Weight gain tendency despite moderate eating
Feeling of undigested food even hours after eating
diet: Light, warm, dry, and easily digestible foods
Small meal portions; avoid overeating completely
Intermittent fasting beneficial (one meal delay)
Warm water with ginger, black pepper, and honey
Mung dal khichdi with warming spices
Avoid heavy dairy, wheat, refined sugar
Bitter greens, radish, mustard greens to stimulate digestion
Use black pepper, dry ginger, pippali, and mustard seeds liberally
Avoid cold foods, refrigerated leftovers, and raw foods
herbs: Trikatu Churna — most important digestive stimulant for Manda Agni
Varanadi Kashayam — abdominal fat and sluggish metabolism
lifestyle: Vigorous exercise daily: jogging, cycling, swimming
Udvartana (dry powder massage) to stimulate metabolism
Wake early before 6 AM; avoid sleeping beyond sunrise
Hot or warm baths to stimulate circulation
Reduce attachment and emotional eating patterns

panchakarma.overview: Panchakarma (Pañcakarma) is the five-fold Ayurvedic bio-purification therapy. It involves Purvakarma (preparatory procedures: Snehana and Svedana) followed by the five main cleansing procedures.

procedures.name: Vamana
contraindication: Children below 7 years and elderly above 80 years
procedures.preparatoryDiet: 3–7 days of Snehana (internal oleation with medicated ghee starting 10–60 ml/day) and Svedana (steam therapy). Patient eats Kapha-promoting foods: milk, sugarcane, sesame. Avoid dry, cold, and raw foods.
procedures.procedure: On the day of Vamana: the patient is given a light meal the previous night. In the morning, after Svedana, they consume Vamana dravyas (emetics): typically Madanaphala (Randia dumetorum) paste with Licorice decoction, rock salt, and honey. Approximately 500–1000 ml is consumed. Vomiting is induced by stimulating the throat with fingers. 4–8 bouts (Vegas) are ideal (Pravara). Vamana is graded as Pravara (best), Madhyama (moderate), or Avara (mild).
procedures.postCare: Samsarjana Krama (post-detox diet): Progressive re-feeding starting with warm rice water (Peya), then thick rice gruel (Vilepi), mung dal soup, soft rice with ghee, gradually returning to normal diet over 7 days. Rest, avoid cold exposure, wind, and exertion.

procedures.name: Virechana
procedures.preparatoryDiet: 7-day Snehana with Tikta Ghrita (bitter medicated ghee) in graduated doses (10–80 ml). Daily Svedana with herbal steam. Meals should be light, warm, and easily digestible. Avoid spicy, sour, and fermented foods.
procedures.procedure: After completion of Snehana and Svedana, Virechana dravya (purgative) is administered on an empty stomach. Commonly used: Trivrit Lehya (Operculina turpethum preparation), Castor oil in milk, Eranda Taila, or Ichhabhedi Rasa. Patient should remain rested. Adequate purgation (Pravara) is 30+ bouts. Graded as Pravara, Madhyama, and Avara according to frequency.
procedures.postCare: Samsarjana Krama as with Vamana. Rest, avoidance of heat, sun, exertion, cold, and emotional stress. Medicated buttermilk (Takra) often used in post-care. Diet progression over 7 days from Peya to normal.

procedures.name: Basti
procedures.targetLocation: Colon, pelvic region, bones, and nervous system
contraindication: Pregnancy (Anuvasana Basti only under expert guidance)
procedures.preparatoryDiet: Light, warm, easily digestible food. Snehana and Svedana for 3–5 days. Meals should be vata-pacifying: warm, oily, well-cooked with ghee.
procedures.procedure: Two main types: (1) Niruha Basti (Asthapana): herbal decoction-based enema with honey, rock salt, oil, herbal paste, and decoction. Retained for 48 minutes ideally then expelled. (2) Anuvasana Basti: medicated oil enema (Sesame or medicated oil, 60–120 ml) retained overnight. Matra Basti is a simplified daily Anuvasana. Karma Basti is a full course of 30 bastis (Niruha + Anuvasana in sequence).
procedures.postCare: Rest after procedure. Warm liquids and light diet. Warm oil application to lower abdomen. Avoid cold, wind, and exertion on day of Basti.

procedures.name: Nasya
procedures.targetDosha: Kapha and Vata (head and neck region)
procedures.targetLocation: Head, neck, sinuses, brain, and sense organs
contraindication: Immediately after meals or on full stomach
Immediately post-bath or during cloudy weather
Children below 7 years for Shodhana Nasya
procedures.preparatoryDiet: Light meal 2–3 hours before Nasya. Local Snehana (oil massage) of head, face, and neck. Local Svedana (steam inhalation or hot towel to face).
procedures.procedure: Patient lies supine with head tilted back over the edge of table. Medicated oil (e.g., Anu Taila, Shadbindu Taila, Panchendriya Vardhan Taila) warmed to body temperature is instilled: 2–8 drops per nostril. Patient remains supine for 2–3 minutes. Expectoration encouraged. Mild gargling with warm water follows. Types: Navana (oily), Avapida (pressed juice), Dhmapana (powder insufflation), Dhuma (smoke inhalation).
procedures.postCare: Avoid cold, wind, and dust after procedure. No exposure to cold water or cold food. Rest for 30 minutes. Avoid speech and exertion immediately after.

procedures.name: Raktamokshana
indication: Skin diseases: psoriasis, chronic eczema, vitiligo
Blood-borne toxins from bites or poisons
procedures.preparatoryDiet: Pitta-pacifying diet for 3–5 days: avoid spicy, sour, fermented foods. Include cooling foods, coconut water, pomegranate.
procedures.procedure: Methods: (1) Shastra Visravana — surgical instruments (venesection, scarification). (2) Anushastra Visravana — non-surgical. Includes Jalaukavacharana (leech therapy — most common in modern practice): medicinal leeches (Hirudo medicinalis or Hirudinaria) applied to affected skin, allowed to feed 30–45 minutes, removed with salt. (3) Shringa (horn), Alabu (gourd), and Ghati Yantra (cupping). Modern leech therapy most practised.
procedures.postCare: Wound care after leech removal: turmeric paste application. Pitta-pacifying diet. Rest for 24 hours. Monitor for infection. Avoid hot and spicy food.

rasayana.overview: Rasayana therapy aims at rejuvenation, longevity, and enhancement of immunity (Ojas). These herbs and formulations nourish the Dhatus (tissues) progressively and restore youthful function.

herbs.prabhava: Rasayana — powerful rejuvenator; balances all three Doshas
herbs.dose: 3–6 g powder or 10–20 ml fresh juice daily
herbs.modernEvidence: Rich in Vitamin C (Ascorbic acid), gallic acid, ellagic acid; strong antioxidant, hepatoprotective, immunomodulatory, anti-diabetic, and anti-atherogenic effects confirmed in multiple RCTs.

herbs.dose: 3–6 g root powder or 300–600 mg standardised extract
herbs.anupana: Warm milk with honey or ghee
herbs.modernEvidence: Withanolides proven to reduce cortisol, improve VO2 max, testosterone, muscle mass, sperm parameters, thyroid function (TSH), and anxiety scores. Multiple double-blind RCTs available.

herbs.dose: 3–6 g powder or 5–10 ml liquid extract
herbs.modernEvidence: Steroidal saponins (Shatavarins) shown to improve fertility markers, oestrogen levels, and galactagogue effect. Adaptogenic and immunomodulatory properties confirmed.

herbs.prabhava: Medhya Rasayana (brain tonic); improves memory and cognitive function
herbs.dose: 300–600 mg standardised extract or 3 g powder
herbs.modernEvidence: Bacosides A and B improve acetylcholine synthesis, reduce beta-amyloid in animal models. Multiple RCTs confirm memory, attention, and anxiety improvement. CDRI-08 extract extensively studied.

herbs.prabhava: Tridosha Shamaka (pacifies all three doshas), Rasayana, Deepana
herbs.dose: 1–3 g powder or 10–20 ml fresh stem decoction
herbs.modernEvidence: Tinosporine, berberine, tinocordiside shown to be immunomodulatory, anti-hyperglycemic, hepatoprotective, anti-inflammatory. Used clinically in dengue and COVID adjunct protocols.

rasa: All six rasas present; Madhura, Amla, Katu, Tikta, Kashaya dominant
herbs.prabhava: Rasayana; considered the King of Medicines in Ayurveda
herbs.dose: 1–3 g powder at bedtime with warm water
herbs.anupana: Warm water; or with ghee, honey, or rock salt depending on indication
herbs.modernEvidence: Chebulinic acid, chebulagic acid, and gallic acid demonstrate strong antimicrobial, antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-diabetic activity. Inhibits HIV reverse transcriptase in vitro.

herbs.dose: 1–3 g powder
herbs.anupana: Honey for respiratory conditions; warm water otherwise
herbs.modernEvidence: Ellagic acid, gallic acid demonstrate antioxidant activity. Gallic acid from Bibhitaki is anti-lipidemic and hepatoprotective. Studies show anti-fungal and anti-bacterial properties.

herbs.dose: 2–4 g powder or 10–15 ml fresh juice
herbs.modernEvidence: Scopoletin and flavonoids shown to have anxiolytic, anti-epileptic, anti-hypertensive, and thyroid-suppressant effects. May reduce phenytoin bioavailability.

herbs.prabhava: Medhya Rasayana; wound healer, collagen stimulator
herbs.dose: 1–3 g powder or 10–20 ml fresh leaf juice
herbs.modernEvidence: Asiaticoside, madecassoside, asiatic acid stimulate collagen synthesis, improve chronic venous insufficiency, enhance memory consolidation. Strong evidence base from multiple RCTs.

herbs.dose: 1–3 g powder; 500–1500 mg standardised curcumin extract
herbs.anupana: Warm milk with black pepper (enhances bioavailability 20×); ghee
herbs.modernEvidence: Curcumin modulates NF-kB, COX-2, and inflammatory cytokines. Bioavailability is poor but improved with piperine. Studies positive for osteoarthritis, IBD, depression (comparable to fluoxetine), and metabolic syndrome.

herbs.dose: 1–2 g leaf powder; 5–10 ml leaf juice
herbs.modernEvidence: Azadirachtin, nimbin, nimbidin show antiparasitic, antifungal, antibacterial, anti-hyperglycemic, and anti-inflammatory activity. Used in natural pesticides and oral hygiene.

herbs.dose: 3–5 g whole plant powder or 10–20 ml decoction
herbs.modernEvidence: Phyllanthin, hypophyllanthin show potent hepatoprotective and anti-HBV activity. Inhibits HBsAg and HBeAg expression. Anti-urolithic activity confirmed in clinical trials. WHO traditional medicine database positive evidence.

herbs.dose: 1–3 g root powder; 400 mg DGL (deglycyrrhizinated licorice) extract
herbs.modernEvidence: Glycyrrhizin (anti-inflammatory, antiviral), glycyrrhetinic acid (anti-ulcer). DGL equivalent to H2 blockers for peptic ulcer. Potent anti-SARS-CoV activity reported. Anti-hepatitis properties studied.

herbs.dose: 250–500 mg guggulsterone-standardised extract twice daily
herbs.anupana: Warm water; or with Triphala for obesity
herbs.modernEvidence: Guggulsterones E and Z inhibit bile acid receptor FXR, reduce LDL. Phase II/III trials showed lipid-lowering effect, though results are mixed for LDL. Anti-inflammatory and thyroid-stimulating effects confirmed.

herbs.dose: 3–5 g root powder or 10–30 ml root decoction
herbs.modernEvidence: Punarnavine, liriodendrin shown to be diuretic, anti-inflammatory, anti-fibrotic (liver), and nephroprotective. Inhibits calcium oxalate crystal formation.

herbs.botanicalName: Combination: Piper longum + Zingiber officinale + Piper nigrum
herbs.dose: 250–500 mg with warm water before meals
herbs.modernEvidence: Piperine (bioperine) from Piper nigrum enhances bioavailability of multiple drugs and nutrients by 20–200%. Shogaols and gingerols from Zingiber are anti-nausea and anti-inflammatory. Well-studied individually.

herbs.prabhava: Tridoshahara (balances all doshas in proper use), Krimighna, Vishaghna
herbs.dose: 2–3 g dried leaf powder or 10–15 ml fresh leaf juice twice daily
herbs.modernEvidence: Eugenol, rosmarinic acid, ursolic acid provide anti-stress, anti-inflammatory, anti-hyperglycemic, antimicrobial, and immunomodulatory effects. Adaptogenic properties comparable to Ashwagandha.

herbs.dose: 3–5 g seed powder; 500 mg standardised L-DOPA extract
herbs.modernEvidence: Contains 4–6% L-DOPA naturally. Clinical trials show improved Parkinson's motor symptoms. Improves testosterone, sperm count, and motility. Anti-diabetic and antioxidant effects confirmed.

herbs.dose: 3–5 g root powder or 20–30 ml root decoction
herbs.anupana: Warm milk or Bala taila for Abhyanga
herbs.modernEvidence: Ephedrine alkaloids (vasicine), mucilage, flavonoids provide bronchodilatory, anti-inflammatory, and anabolic properties. Stem bark extract studied for cardiac protective effect.

herbs.botanicalName: Combination: Terminalia chebula + T. bellirica + Phyllanthus emblica
rasa: Five of the six tastes present; lacks Lavana (salt)
herbs.dose: 3–5 g powder at bedtime with warm water
herbs.anupana: Warm water; honey for Kapha; ghee for Vata; sugar for Pitta
herbs.modernEvidence: Most researched Ayurvedic poly-herbal formulation. Antioxidant, anti-diabetic, anti-obesity, anti-carcinogenic, hepatoprotective effects confirmed. Multiple clinical trials for constipation, IBS, and colitis.

siddha.overview: Siddha medicine is one of the oldest traditional systems, developed by the Tamil Siddhas. It operates on the three humors — Vatham, Pitham, Kapham — and five elements (Pancha Bootham). Diagnosis includes Naadi Pariksha (pulse diagnosis), Naa Pariksha (tongue), and Kann Pariksha (eye).

subtypes.name: Dhananjayan
subtypes.function: Present in body even after death; decomposition

vatham.qualitiesWhenBalanced: Normal movement, cognition, elimination, breathing, and circulation
vatham.qualitiesWhenImbalanced: Pain, spasm, paralysis, constipation, dryness, anxiety, tremors

pitham.qualitiesWhenBalanced: Good digestion, fair complexion, normal body heat, clarity of vision and intellect
pitham.qualitiesWhenImbalanced: Fever, acidity, jaundice, bleeding disorders, skin inflammation, anger, excessive hunger

subtypes.name: Avalambagam
subtypes.function: Nourishes all other Kaphams; supports heart

kapham.qualitiesWhenBalanced: Good immunity, stability, lubrication, strength, compassion
kapham.qualitiesWhenImbalanced: Edema, obesity, mucus congestion, sluggishness, depression, diabetes

formulations.caution: Avoid in Pitta disorders, peptic ulcer, pregnancy
Avoid during pregnancy; monitor liver function in prolonged use

formulations.dose: External application; 5–10 ml for topical use
formulations.caution: Avoid in cold, Vata conditions
Avoid in hyperthyroidism; reduce dose in Pitta disorders

formulations.dose: 65–130 mg (1–2 ratti) with honey or butter
formulations.caution: Must be prepared under strict Siddha pharmacy protocols; heavy metal content — use under qualified practitioner supervision only

formulations.dose: External application to affected area; 2–5 ml
formulations.caution: Do not apply on open wounds; do not ingest

formulations.anupana: Warm water; honey for eye diseases
formulations.caution: Reduce dose in diarrhea; avoid excess in pregnancy
Avoid on sensitive or inflamed skin
Use cautiously in bradycardia

formulations.tamilName: Kasthuri Manjal Lepam / கஸ்தூரி மஞ்சள் லேபம்
formulations.dose: External application; thin paste on face for 15–20 minutes
formulations.caution: Patch test before use; avoid sensitive skin
Avoid in estrogen-dependent conditions

formulations.anupana: Honey or butter with Triphala decoction
formulations.caution: Use under qualified Siddha practitioner; avoid in hemochromatosis

formulations.dose: Infants: 50–100 mg with mother's milk; Adults: 1–2 g
formulations.anupana: Honey or mother's milk for infants
formulations.caution: Use with caution in seizure disorder; external application on abdomen for infant colic
Monitor blood sugar in diabetics due to sugar content
Avoid in dry, Vata-type cough without mucus
Avoid with anticoagulants

formulations.dose: 3–5 g in milk or water twice daily
formulations.caution: Monitor blood sugar; safe in most conditions

formulations.tamilName: Piper Longum Lehyam / Thippili Rasayanam / திப்பிலி ரசாயனம்
formulations.caution: Avoid in Pitta excess, hyperacidity

formulations.dose: External application twice daily on cleaned wound
formulations.caution: Do not apply on infected wounds without debridement
Strictly under qualified Siddha physician; contains heavy metals — shodhana purification mandatory
Avoid in pregnancy; prolonged use may affect liver enzymes

formulations.dose: 15–30 ml diluted in equal water twice daily
formulations.caution: Reduce dose in hyperacidity; long-term use needs monitoring

formulations.tamilName: Asthi Poshak Churna / அஸ்தி போஷக சூரணம்
formulations.caution: Avoid in hypercalcemia; use under guidance
Generally safe; avoid excessive use in Pitta disorders
Avoid in pregnancy; caution with anticoagulants
Avoid in hyperthyroidism; contraindicated in pregnancy
Monitor electrolytes in prolonged use; avoid with potassium-sparing diuretics

formulations.tamilName: Swarna Makshika Bhasma / ஸ்வர்ண மாக்ஷிக பஸ்மம்
formulations.caution: Strictly shodhita (purified) preparations only; use under qualified physician

formulations.tamilName: Siddha Makarathvaja Chendooram / சித்த மகரத்துவஜ செந்தூரம்
formulations.caution: Most potent Siddha Rasayana; strictly qualified practitioner only; not for self-medication; regulatory compliance required
Avoid in constipation; reduce dose if stools become too firm

nadiPariksha.instructions: Naadi Pariksha (நாடி பரிட்சை) is performed on the radial artery at the wrist. The patient should be in a relaxed seated position. Examination is done in the morning on an empty stomach, after bodily hygiene, and not after exercise, meals, or emotional disturbance. The index, middle, and ring fingers of the physician are placed on the radial pulse approximately 1 cm proximal to the wrist crease, with gentle but firm pressure.

patterns.movement: Slithering, irregular, zigzag movement like a cobra
patterns.clinicalCorrelation: Neurological disorders, anxiety, constipation, joint disorders, Vata diseases

patterns.movement: Jumping, skipping, active, forceful beats with irregular rhythm
patterns.clinicalCorrelation: Fever, inflammatory conditions, liver disorders, hyperacidity, skin diseases
Obesity, diabetes, respiratory congestion, sluggish digestion, edema
Healthy individual; balanced tridosha state; found in advanced Siddhas

patterns.movement: Sucking, drawing-in movement with variable depth
patterns.clinicalCorrelation: Blood disorders, inflammatory neurological conditions, bleeding tendencies

patterns.movement: Quick, pecking, sharp then slow beats
patterns.clinicalCorrelation: Respiratory-digestive combined disorders, bronchial asthma with digestive weakness

patterns.movement: Scurrying, uneven, alternately quick and sluggish
patterns.clinicalCorrelation: Mucus-related neurological disorders, hypothyroid with anxiety, chronic sinusitis

ahara.overview: Ahara (Aahaaram / ஆகாரம்) — Food as Medicine. According to both Ayurveda and Siddha, the quality of food not only affects the body (Sthula Sharira) but also the mind (Sukshma Sharira) and consciousness. Food is classified by Guna (quality) and Virya (potency).
satvicFoods: Fresh fruits: mango, pomegranate, fig, grapes, banana
Leafy greens: spinach, methi (fenugreek), moringa, curry leaves
Cooked vegetables: pumpkin, sweet potato, zucchini, bottle gourd
Whole grains: brown rice, oats, barley, millet
Legumes: mung dal, toor dal, chana dal (well-cooked)
Dairy: fresh cow's milk, ghee, fresh curd (in moderation)
Nuts and seeds: soaked almonds, sesame, flaxseed, walnuts
Natural sweeteners: raw honey, jaggery (in moderation)
Herbs and spices: turmeric, cumin, coriander, fennel, saffron
Water: pure spring or filtered water at room temperature
rajasicFoods: Spicy, pungent, and heavily seasoned food
Fermented foods: alcohol, vinegar, aged cheese
Fast food and restaurant food cooked in refined oils
tamasicFoods: Heavily processed and refined food products

foods: Bitter gourd (Karavellai) — 50 ml juice on empty stomach
Fenugreek seeds (Venthayam) — 1 tsp soaked overnight, taken on empty stomach
Neem leaves (Veppam) — 5–7 leaves on empty stomach
Turmeric milk (Manjal paal) — at bedtime
Barley water and whole barley
Green leafy vegetables: spinach, drumstick leaves, curry leaves
Low glycemic fruits: Jamun (Naval pazham), pomegranate, papaya
Brown rice or millets: kambu, cholam, ragi, thinai
Cinnamon water — 1/2 tsp cinnamon boiled in water
Amla (Nelli) — fresh juice 20 ml daily
avoid: Refined white rice, white bread, maida products
Sweet fruits: mango, banana, dates, grapes in excess
therapeuticDiets.timing: Three measured meals at fixed times. No snacking. Dinner before 7 PM. Walk 30 minutes after meals.
therapeuticDiets.notes: Emphasise low glycemic index millets as staple. Include 30 minutes of Yoga (Sarvangasana, Ardha Matsyendrasana, Pranayama) daily. Monitor fasting and post-prandial blood glucose weekly.

foods: Garlic: 2–3 raw cloves daily on empty stomach
Celery juice: 100 ml daily
Watermelon: regular intake (citrulline content)
Flaxseed: 1 tbsp ground daily in food
Beetroot juice: 100–150 ml daily
Potassium-rich foods: banana, avocado, coconut water, spinach
Magnesium-rich foods: pumpkin seeds, dark leafy greens
Dark chocolate (>70%): small amounts
Hibiscus tea (Sembaruthi tea): twice daily
Pomegranate juice: 100 ml daily
therapeuticDiets.timing: DASH-aligned dietary pattern. Small meals 4–5 times daily. Avoid heavy dinner. No meals 2 hours before sleep.
therapeuticDiets.notes: DASH diet principles apply. Emphasise low-salt cooking using lemon, herbs, and spices for flavour. Yoga: Shavasan, Anulom Vilom, slow walking. Regular blood pressure monitoring.

therapeuticDiets.condition: Obesity and Metabolic Syndrome (Sthaulya / Kobesity / உடல் பருமன்)
foods: Warm lemon water with honey on empty stomach (morning)
Millets as staple: ragi, kambu, varagu, thinai
High-fibre vegetables: cabbage, cauliflower, broccoli, spinach
Moringa (Murungai) — leaves, pods daily
Apple cider vinegar: 1 tsp in warm water before meals
Green tea or tulsi tea between meals
Coconut: in moderate amounts (MCT benefit)
Protein at every meal: dal, sprouts, buttermilk, eggs
Triphala at bedtime
Trikatu Churna before meals
avoid: Refined carbohydrates: white rice, white flour, bread, pasta
therapeuticDiets.timing: Intermittent fasting pattern: 16:8 or 12:12. Largest meal at lunch. Light dinner before 7 PM. No eating after sunset. Warm water throughout day.
therapeuticDiets.notes: Udwartana (dry herbal massage) weekly. Daily vigorous exercise. Avoid daytime sleep. Panchakarma: Lekhana Basti for stubborn obesity. Weekly 1-day fruit or mung dal mono-diet fast.

therapeuticDiets.condition: Acid Reflux and Gastritis (Amlapitta / Vayitru Eri / வயிற்றெரிச்சல்)
foods: Coconut water: 200 ml twice daily (most effective soothing agent)
Cold milk (plain, non-flavoured): 100 ml for acute symptoms
Banana: ripe, easy on stomach lining
Bottle gourd (Suraikkai): cooked and soup
Sabja seeds (Falooda seeds / Ocimum basilicum) in water
Aloe vera juice: 20–30 ml on empty stomach
Licorice root tea: twice daily
Fennel seeds (Sombu): chewed after meals or as tea
Amalaki (Nelli) juice: 20 ml in morning
Lukewarm (not hot) food and beverages
avoid: Eating too fast or too much
Lying down within 2 hours of meals
therapeuticDiets.timing: Five small meals rather than three large ones. Eat slowly and chew well. Do not skip meals. Elevate head of bed if nighttime symptoms present.
therapeuticDiets.notes: Yashtimadhu (DGL licorice) 400 mg before meals. Avipattikar Churna 3 g in cold water at bedtime. Avoid NSAIDs. Vajrasana posture for 15 minutes post-meals.

therapeuticDiets.condition: Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS) (Grahani / Kuthal Noi / குதல் நோய்)
foods: Khichdi (rice + mung dal with ghee): digestive rest food
Well-cooked vegetables: carrot, zucchini, bottle gourd, pumpkin
Banana and cooked apple (astringent for diarrhea type)
Warm fennel and cumin tea after meals
Psyllium husk (Isabgol): 1–2 tsp in warm water at bedtime
Buttermilk with roasted cumin and ginger (Takram)
Pomegranate: anti-diarrheal, anti-inflammatory
Ginger tea: anti-spasmodic and carminative
Rice water (Kanji): during flares
Probiotic yogurt (fresh): 100 ml at room temperature
avoid: Beans and legumes (except split mung dal)
therapeuticDiets.timing: Regular, fixed meal times. Small portions 4–5 times daily. Avoid rushing meals. 15-minute rest after meals. Avoid meals under stress.
therapeuticDiets.notes: FODMAP elimination trial beneficial. Hingwashtak Churna before meals for Vata-IBS. Kutajarishta 15 ml after meals for diarrhea-predominant. Triphala for constipation-predominant. Yoga: Pawanmuktasana, Ardha Matsyendrasana.

therapeuticDiets.condition: Rheumatoid Arthritis (Amavata / Keel Vayu / கீல் வாயு)
foods: Castor oil: 5–10 ml at bedtime 2–3 times weekly (Vata pacifying)
Anti-inflammatory spices: turmeric, ginger, garlic, fenugreek
Omega-3 rich foods: flaxseeds, chia, walnuts, fatty fish (if non-veg)
Moringa (Murungai) daily — anti-inflammatory
Pomegranate juice and berries (antioxidant)
Warm soups and stews
Ginger tea multiple times daily
Sesame seeds: anti-arthritic Vata-pacifying
Bitter gourd: purifies blood
Green moong dal: light, anti-ama (anti-toxic)
avoid: Cold, raw foods and cold beverages
Nightshades: tomato, potato, brinjal (eggplant), bell pepper
Beans and legumes other than mung dal
therapeuticDiets.timing: Warm, cooked meals at fixed times. Avoid cold, refrigerated food. Light dinner. Fasting (Langhan) during flares on warm liquids only.
therapeuticDiets.notes: Ama Pachana (toxin digestion) using Trikatu and Pippali is primary approach. Dashamularishta 15 ml post meals. Panchakarma: Virechana + Basti. Yoga: gentle joint mobility exercises, swimming. Avoid excessive cold.

therapeuticDiets.condition: Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome / PCOS (Kaphodbhava Artava Kshaya / பெண் நீர்க்கட்டி)
foods: Methi seeds (Fenugreek): 1 tsp soaked overnight, eat in morning
Cinnamon: 1/2 tsp in warm water daily (improves insulin sensitivity)
Flaxseeds: 1 tbsp ground daily (lignans balance estrogen)
Spearmint tea: twice daily (anti-androgenic effect — reduces testosterone)
Berries: anti-inflammatory, low glycemic
Millets as staple (low GI)
Amla (Nelli): 20 ml juice daily
Shatavari: Rasayana for female reproductive system
Turmeric milk: reduces insulin resistance
Moringa leaves: iron, calcium, hormonal balance
avoid: Dairy especially milk and cheese (IGF-1 connection)
therapeuticDiets.timing: Low glycemic diet throughout. Intermittent fasting beneficial (consult physician). Regular meal timings. No late-night eating.
therapeuticDiets.notes: Kanchanara Guggulu and Shatavari as primary herbal support. Exercise: 150 min/week vigorous. Yoga: Baddha Konasana, Navasana. Weight management crucial — even 5% reduction improves menstrual regularity.

therapeuticDiets.condition: Hypothyroidism (Manda Agni + Kapha Vridhi / Thayiroid Kuzhandru / தைராய்டு குழந்து)
foods: Seaweed and iodine-rich foods: seafood, iodised salt
Selenium-rich foods: Brazil nuts (2/day), sunflower seeds
Ashwagandha (Amukkara): adaptogen, improves TSH
Guggulu: thyroid-stimulating effect
Ginger: metabolic stimulant
Coconut oil: MCT stimulates metabolism
Zinc-rich foods: pumpkin seeds, sesame, dark chocolate
Vitamin D: egg yolk, mushrooms, sunlight
Iron-rich foods: moringa, pomegranate, ragi
Fermented foods in moderation: idli, dosa (for gut health)
avoid: Raw cruciferous vegetables: raw cabbage, broccoli, cauliflower (goitrogenic when raw — cooked is fine)
therapeuticDiets.timing: Three regular warm meals. Thyroid medication (if prescribed) to be taken 30–60 minutes before breakfast, away from calcium and iron supplements.
therapeuticDiets.notes: Kanchanara Guggulu — primary Ayurvedic thyroid support. Vigorous morning exercise. Avoid daytime sleep. Manda Agni diet principles apply. Yoga: Sarvangasana (shoulder stand), Halasana, Matsyasana.

therapeuticDiets.condition: Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD) (Mutra Vaha Srotas Vikara / சிறுநீரக நோய்)
foods: Punarnava (Mukaratte): primary diuretic herb — 30 ml decoction
Keezhanelli (Phyllanthus niruri): nephroprotective
Barley water: soothing and diuretic
Cucumber and ridge gourd: alkalising and gentle diuretic
Rice water (Kanji): easy-to-digest staple
Coconut water (in early stages when K+ is normal)
Drumstick soup (Murungai): anti-inflammatory
Cranberry juice: prevents UTI-related progression
Bottle gourd: hydrating and anti-inflammatory
Red rice or white rice in preference to millets (lower potassium)
avoid: High potassium foods in advanced CKD: banana, potato, tomato, orange, dried fruits
High phosphorus foods: dairy, nuts, seeds, colas
Excess protein (limit to 0.6–0.8 g/kg/day in CKD 3–5)
NSAIDs and nephrotoxic herbs: excess Punarnava, aristolochic acid plants
Processed and canned foods (high sodium and phosphate)
therapeuticDiets.timing: Low protein diet; spread protein across 3 meals. Fluid restriction per nephrologist advice. No fasting without medical supervision in CKD 3+.
therapeuticDiets.notes: Must be integrated with nephrological care. Regular renal function tests, potassium, phosphorus monitoring. Chandraprabha Vati, Gokshuradi Guggulu under physician guidance. Avoid aggressive Panchakarma in advanced CKD.

therapeuticDiets.condition: Insomnia and Sleep Disorders (Anidra / Thookkamillai / தூக்கமில்லை)
foods: Warm milk with nutmeg (Jaiphal) and cardamom 1 hour before sleep
Ashwagandha milk: 5 g Ashwagandha in warm milk at bedtime
Banana: tryptophan and magnesium content
Tart cherry juice: melatonin-rich
Walnuts: melatonin and omega-3
Chamomile tea: 1 cup 30 minutes before bed
Brahmi tea: nervine tonic
Magnesium-rich foods: pumpkin seeds, dark chocolate, spinach
Light, easy-to-digest dinner: khichdi, vegetable soup
Warm sesame oil foot massage before sleep
avoid: Caffeine after 2 PM (coffee, tea, cola, energy drinks)
Heavy dinner especially protein and fat-heavy meals
Stimulating screen exposure 1 hour before bedtime
therapeuticDiets.timing: Dinner by 7 PM minimum. Fixed sleep time; wake time regardless of sleep duration. Avoid meals within 2 hours of sleep. Establish Dinacharya (fixed daily routine).
therapeuticDiets.notes: Abhyanga with Bala taila or Brahmi taila for head massage. Shankhapushpi + Ashwagandha combination for anxiety-related insomnia. Jatamansi (Nardostachys jatamansi) 1 g at bedtime. Yoga Nidra 20–30 minutes at bedtime. Avoid naps during day.
